originalBehavior: The published article walks readers through changing classification thresholds and shows how each threshold alters true-positive and false-positive outcomes before the full ROC curve is introduced.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original scroll-linked threshold sweep scenes, highlighted threshold point, and linked explanatory copy from the compiled app.
notes: The route still mounts from the original compiled bundle into an otherwise empty document body.
Only metadata and public-shell branding were patched around the app.
evidence: The implementation plan treats threshold-point movement on the ROC curve as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage will advance the route and verify the active ROC threshold point changes.

originalBehavior: The article builds the ROC curve from threshold examples, then compares the model curve against baseline and ideal references to explain what makes a strong classifier.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original ROC chart rendering, random and perfect comparison lines, and the published comparison scene ordering without rewriting the compiled logic.
notes: The original chart IDs and line layers were preserved so the published line comparisons still render in place.
No new comparison scenes were invented beyond what the live page ships.
evidence: The implementation plan explicitly calls out ROC curve rendering and classifier comparison if the live page ships one.
Shared smoke coverage will verify the main ROC scene initializes and the comparison layers render without runtime errors.

originalBehavior: Later scenes explain the area under the ROC curve with shaded or highlighted overlays and accompanying interpretation text about classifier quality.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original AUC chart, shaded interpretation region, and explanation text from the published bundle.
notes: The published route does not expose standalone slider controls in the public body; the threshold and AUC scenes are scroll-linked.
The local route preserves that structure rather than inventing new controls.
evidence: The implementation plan treats AUC shading or interpretation overlays as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage will verify the AUC scene renders and its highlighted interpretation state appears as the page advances.

originalBehavior: The published route included MLU branding, creator bylines, analytics, repo and promo links, and contributor callouts inside the public body.
localStatus: The local route removes those public-shell references, localizes the remaining navigation, and uses the shared footer as the single live original-page reference surface.
notes: Docs retain the upstream repo and provenance notes.
The public page no longer exposes creator or project promo surfaces outside the footer.
evidence: The public-surface audit blocks creator names, MLU branding, upstream domains, promo links, and contributor callouts in shipped route code.
The route now advertises only the shared footer link back to the live original page.
